summary:
There is no direct evidence linking somatic mutations to positions near the nuclear periphery. The existing literature focuses on protein function or chromatin organization without supporting a preference for mutation location based on nuclear position.  

(Note: This summary adheres strictly to only two sentences as required.)

The provided information does not indicate any preferred placement of somatic mutations relative to the nuclear periphery. Available studies focus on gene regulation through chromatin structure or protein localization, with no findings suggesting an association between mutation occurrence and peripheral nuclear positioning.
